summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the vulnerable Zip::extract() method allows ZIP entries with path traversal sequences (../) to be extracted outside the intended target directory, confirming the path traversal vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: The proof-of-concept demonstrates arbitrary file write by creating a malicious ZIP with '../../../outside_target/poc_shell.php' entry. The file is extracted outside the extraction directory, proving that attackers can write files to arbitrary locations on the filesystem.
actual_observed_result: Running the verification script successfully demonstrated that ZIP entries with '../' sequences escape the extraction directory. The file /var/www/outside_target/poc_shell.php was created outside the intended /var/www/html/tmp extraction directory, confirming the path traversal vulnerability.
verification_method: The verification script creates a malicious ZIP with path traversal entries, simulates the vulnerable extraction process, and checks whether files were written outside the target directory. The presence of poc_shell.php in /var/www/outside_target confirms successful path traversal.
evidence_produced_by_run: Extracted file appeared at /var/www/outside_target/poc_shell.php (outside extraction directory)
File contains PHP webshell payload: <?php echo 'EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS_MARKER'; ?>
This demonstrates that Zip::extract() does not validate entry paths before extraction
No call to realpath() or path validation to prevent '../' sequences
limitations_or_blockers: Full exploitation requires an authenticated session with upload access
The target repository requires database setup for complete functionality
This PoC simulates the vulnerable code path to prove path traversal exists

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
